judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 1 fully satisfies all four expectations, including explicitly endorsing a going-forward same-commit doc policy and recommending dropping the hardcoded webhook count in favor of pointing at the source; Report 2 hits (a) and (b) but falls short on (c)'s going-forward endorsement and (d)'s recommendation to stop hardcoding the count, merely proposing to update "4" to "5".

report_1.evidence: New refund calls should go in medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py... found two places, beyond the documented errors.py exception, that construct their own chargekit.Client and call the provider directly instead of going through gateway.py: medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4... medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2...

report_2.evidence: New calls should live in medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py... The only sanctioned exception is medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6, which imports chargekit.errors for exception classes — and that matches what the doc describes.

report_1.evidence: InvoiceOut (medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22) has a sixth field, external_ref... This field was added in migration 0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql

report_2.evidence: The actual InvoiceOut dataclass (medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22) now has 6 fields — the same 5 plus external_ref. This field was added in commit c23da88... which touched invoices.py, invoice.py, and a new migration (0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql)

report_1.evidence: drift between the contract doc and the code is a bug, not a "someday" cleanup item, and any change to a documented invariant must update the doc in the same commit as the code change... Going forward, any commit that adds a refund accessor to gateway.py, or touches InvoiceOut, or adds/removes a webhook branch, must update docs/ARCHITECTURE.md in that same commit, per project documentation's core rule.

report_2.evidence: Timing: this should happen now, as part of the refund PR (or immediately before it) rather than deferred — the doc is what the person picking up refund work will read first, and both discrepancies predate this feature.

report_1.evidence: rather than just correcting "4" to "5" and adding payment_pending (a fix that will silently go stale again the next time someone adds a branch...), state the invariant so it can't drift unnoticed... either point at the source of truth ("see the kind == branches in app/api/webhooks.py for the current list of handled event types") or... pair it with a lightweight enforcement mechanism

report_2.evidence: I would change the doc to say "5 webhook event types" and add payment_pending to the list.
